Pattern Recognition Guide

Map problem keywords to algorithm patterns — the fastest way to crack coding interviews.

Table of Contents

  • How to Use This Page
  • Keyword → Pattern Map
  • Array / String Keywords
  • Graph / Tree Keywords
  • Dynamic Programming Keywords
  • Backtracking / Recursion Keywords
  • Design / Data Structure Keywords
  • Greedy Keywords
  • Bit Manipulation Keywords
  • Decision Flowchart
  • Quick Pattern Comparison

Pattern Recognition Guide

See a keyword in the problem? Jump to the right pattern instantly. This page maps problem signals to algorithm patterns — the fastest way to crack coding interviews.

How to Use This Page

  1. Read the problem statement
  2. Spot keywords or structural clues below
  3. Jump to the matching pattern and its template

Keyword → Pattern Map

Array / String Keywords

Keyword / Clue in Problem Pattern Example Problems Cheat Sheet
“subarray sum equals k”, “sum of subarray” Prefix Sum LC 560, 974, 523, 1248 prefix_sum
“contiguous subarray”, “maximum subarray” Kadane’s / Sliding Window LC 53, 918, 152 kadane
“longest substring without repeating”, “at most k distinct” Sliding Window LC 3, 159, 340, 424, 76 sliding_window
“minimum window”, “smallest subarray” Sliding Window (shrink) LC 76, 209, 862 sliding_window
“sorted array”, “find target”, “search” Binary Search LC 704, 33, 153, 34 binary_search
“minimum/maximum possible value”, “capacity”, “can you achieve” Binary Search on Answer LC 875, 1011, 410, 1283 binary_search
“two sum”, “pair with sum”, “three sum” Two Pointers / HashMap LC 1, 15, 167, 18 2_pointers
“remove duplicates”, “move zeroes”, “sort colors” Two Pointers (in-place) LC 26, 283, 75, 27 2_pointers
“merge intervals”, “overlapping intervals” Interval Sorting LC 56, 57, 253, 435 intervals
“next greater element”, “daily temperatures” Monotonic Stack LC 496, 739, 84, 42 monotonic_stack
“median”, “top k”, “kth largest” Heap / Quick Select LC 215, 295, 347, 973 heap
“difference”, “range update”, “increment subarray” Difference Array LC 370, 1109, 1094 difference_array

Graph / Tree Keywords

Keyword / Clue in Problem Pattern Example Problems Cheat Sheet
“number of islands”, “connected components”, “flood fill” BFS / DFS on Grid LC 200, 695, 733, 130 bfs, dfs
“shortest path” (unweighted) BFS LC 127, 752, 994, 1091 bfs
“shortest path” (weighted, non-negative) Dijkstra LC 743, 787, 1631 Dijkstra
“shortest path” (negative weights) Bellman-Ford LC 787 (k stops) graph
“course schedule”, “prerequisites”, “ordering” Topological Sort LC 207, 210, 269, 1136 topology_sorting
“detect cycle” (directed) Topological Sort / DFS coloring LC 207, 802 topology_sorting
“detect cycle” (undirected) Union-Find LC 684, 685 union_find
“connected”, “groups”, “redundant connection” Union-Find LC 547, 684, 721, 1319 union_find
“lowest common ancestor”, “LCA” DFS / Binary Lifting LC 236, 235, 1644 tree
“level order”, “zigzag”, “right side view” BFS (level-by-level) LC 102, 103, 199, 515 bfs
“serialize/deserialize tree” BFS or Preorder DFS LC 297, 449 tree
“diameter”, “max path sum” DFS (post-order + global max) LC 543, 124, 687 tree
“validate BST”, “inorder” DFS (in-order traversal) LC 98, 230, 99 bst

Dynamic Programming Keywords

Keyword / Clue in Problem Pattern Example Problems Cheat Sheet
“minimum/maximum cost”, “minimum number of” DP (optimization) LC 322, 64, 120, 746 dp
“how many ways”, “number of ways”, “count paths” DP (counting) LC 62, 63, 70, 96 dp
“can you”, “is it possible”, “true or false” DP (feasibility) / Backtracking LC 139, 416, 97, 55 dp
“longest increasing subsequence” DP + Binary Search LC 300, 354, 1143 dp
“edit distance”, “transform”, “common subsequence” Two-String Grid DP LC 72, 1143, 97, 115 dp
“palindrome partitioning”, “palindrome subsequence” Palindrome DP LC 5, 131, 516, 647 palindrome
“knapsack”, “subset sum”, “partition equal” 0/1 Knapsack DP LC 416, 494, 474, 1049 dp
“coin change”, “combinations that sum to” Unbounded Knapsack DP LC 322, 518, 377 dp
“buy and sell stock” State Machine DP LC 121, 122, 123, 188, 309 stock_trading
“word break”, “can segment” DP + Trie/HashSet LC 139, 140, 472 dp
“house robber”, “non-adjacent” Linear DP (take/skip) LC 198, 213, 337, 740 dp

Backtracking / Recursion Keywords

Keyword / Clue in Problem Pattern Example Problems Cheat Sheet
“all combinations”, “all permutations”, “generate all” Backtracking LC 46, 47, 77, 78, 90 backtrack
“subsets”, “power set” Backtracking / Bit Manipulation LC 78, 90, 784 backtrack
“word search”, “exist in board” Backtracking (grid) LC 79, 212 backtrack
“generate parentheses”, “valid combinations” Backtracking (pruning) LC 22, 17, 39 backtrack
“N-Queens”, “Sudoku” Backtracking (constraint) LC 51, 37 backtrack

Design / Data Structure Keywords

Keyword / Clue in Problem Pattern Example Problems Cheat Sheet
“implement LRU cache” HashMap + Doubly Linked List LC 146 design
“implement LFU cache” HashMap + Min-Heap / Freq Map LC 460 design
“implement trie”, “prefix search”, “autocomplete” Trie LC 208, 211, 212, 648 trie
“random”, “shuffle”, “pick random” Reservoir Sampling / HashMap LC 380, 384, 398 design
“iterator”, “flatten”, “next/hasNext” Stack-based Iterator LC 173, 341, 284 iterator
“stream”, “moving average”, “sliding window median” Heap / Queue LC 295, 346, 480 heap

Greedy Keywords

Keyword / Clue in Problem Pattern Example Problems Cheat Sheet
“jump game”, “minimum jumps” Greedy (furthest reach) LC 55, 45, 1024 greedy
“assign cookies”, “meeting rooms” Greedy (sort + scan) LC 455, 253, 435 greedy
“task scheduler”, “reorganize string” Greedy + Heap LC 621, 767 greedy
“gas station”, “circular” Greedy (running sum) LC 134 greedy

Bit Manipulation Keywords

Keyword / Clue in Problem Pattern Example Problems Cheat Sheet
“single number”, “appears once” XOR LC 136, 137, 260 bit_manipulation
“power of two”, “count bits” Bit tricks LC 191, 231, 338 bit_manipulation
“subsets” (alternative) Bitmask enumeration LC 78, 784 bit_manipulation

Decision Flowchart

Is input SORTED or can you sort it?
├── YES → Is it "find target"? → Binary Search
│         Is it "find pair"? → Two Pointers
│         Is it "merge intervals"? → Sort + Scan
│         Is it "find min/max possible"? → Binary Search on Answer
│
├── NO → Is it a GRAPH/GRID?
│        ├── Unweighted shortest path → BFS
│        ├── Weighted shortest path → Dijkstra
│        ├── Dependencies/ordering → Topological Sort
│        ├── Connected components → Union-Find / DFS
│        └── All paths / combinations → DFS + Backtracking
│
├── Is it asking for OPTIMIZATION (min/max)?
│   ├── Overlapping subproblems → DP
│   ├── Local choice = global optimal → Greedy
│   └── Not sure → Try Greedy first, verify with DP
│
├── Is it asking for ALL combinations/permutations?
│   └── Backtracking
│
├── Is it a SUBARRAY/SUBSTRING problem?
│   ├── Sum related → Prefix Sum or Sliding Window
│   ├── Longest/shortest with condition → Sliding Window
│   └── Next greater/smaller → Monotonic Stack
│
└── Is it a DESIGN problem?
    ├── Cache → HashMap + LinkedList
    ├── Prefix operations → Trie
    └── Stream/online → Heap or Queue

Quick Pattern Comparison

When you see TWO patterns that could work How to choose
BFS vs DFS BFS for shortest path / level-order; DFS for exhaustive search / path finding
DP vs Greedy Greedy if local optimal = global optimal; DP if choices depend on subproblems
DP vs Backtracking DP for counting / optimization; Backtracking for enumerating all solutions
HashMap vs Sorting HashMap for O(n) time, O(n) space; Sorting for O(n log n) time, O(1) space
Binary Search vs Two Pointers Binary Search on sorted single array; Two Pointers for pair/triplet problems
Union-Find vs DFS Union-Find for dynamic connectivity; DFS for static graph traversal
Heap vs Sorting Heap for streaming / top-k; Sorting when you need full order
Prefix Sum vs Sliding Window Prefix Sum for any subarray sum query; Sliding Window for contiguous with constraint

CS_basics - Computer Science Fundamentals & Interview Preparation

GitHub • Documentation • Report Issues
